mainEntity.name: I’ve got a limited budget—what’s the most cost‑effective way to start getting cited by voice assistants?

acceptedAnswer.text:
On a tight budget, focus on the highest‑impact basics: clean local listings, a single well‑structured FAQ page, and simple schema via plugins or generators. You don’t need custom tools; you need clear, answer‑friendly content.

Voice assistants care more about quality and structure than about how fancy your implementation stack is.

KEY FACTORS
- Free optimizations: Google Business, Apple Business, Bing Places
- DIY FAQ page with 20–30 real questions and 40–60 word answers
- Low‑cost schema plugins for FAQ and LocalBusiness
- Regular manual testing using your phone’s assistants

Treat this as a focused sprint: one afternoon for local listings, a few evenings for FAQ content, and a short session to add schema. Then iterate only on pages that show early traction.

This keeps you competitive in voice without turning optimization into an expensive science project.
acceptedAnswer.description: With a tight budget, do three things: perfect your free local listings, build one strong FAQ page with clear 40–60 word answers, and use simple plugins to add FAQ/LocalBusiness schema. Then manually test voice queries and refine only the pages that start getting traction.
